summary: Gruber reports that Apple has acquired the domain me.com, citing DNS evidence that the domain was transferred to MarkMonitor (Apple's domain registrar) on the day of the post. He assembles multiple technical clues — whois records, DNS SOA records, and Netcraft reports — all pointing to Apple as the new owner. The domain was previously used by Snappville, a social network builder. Gruber notes that Apple also parked mobileme.com at MarkMonitor, but suggests me.com is the better choice.
key_points: Apple acquired me.com, as evidenced by its parking at MarkMonitor, Apple's domain management company
Multiple DNS records (whois, SOA, Netcraft) independently confirm Apple's ownership of the domain
The domain name server changed the same day, suggesting a fresh acquisition timed ahead of an announcement
Apple also registered mobileme.com but me.com is the shorter, punchier option
money_quotes: "mobileme.com" is parked at MarkMonitor, too, but "me.com" is a bit punchier, no?
key_insight: DNS records and domain registrar evidence reveal Apple quietly acquired me.com, likely for a rebranding of its online services ahead of WWDC.
